page: Vultr voc-m-16c-128gb-1600s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server instance featuring an x86_64 AMD EPYC-Rome processor. The hardware configuration includes 16 vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with a single thread per core, operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The processor features 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 16 MB of L3 cache. This instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is provided via a bundled 1600 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core performance alongside balanced multi-core capabilities. In stress-ng single-core CPU testing, the instance achieves a top-tier rating in the top 10% of tested systems. Multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and overall PassMark CPU scores fall within the average tier. Memory subsystem performance is a notable strength, with PassMark memory scores ranking in the top 25% of systems, supported by a read-only small block cached memory bandwidth of 2241.95 GB/sec and large block uncached write bandwidth of 67.64 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET benchmarks demonstrate average performance, with Redis handling 30.71 million operations per second.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the instance offers high memory density and substantial local NVMe SSD storage relative to its compute resources. However, the CPU allocation is shared, which introduces potential performance variability under sustained multi-tenant workloads. The high memory capacity of 128 GB combined with fast local storage makes this instance well-suited for memory-intensive workloads, database hosting, caching layers, and general-purpose applications requiring large datasets but moderate, burstable CPU utilization.
description: Vultr voc-m-16c-128gb-1600s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance powered by an x86_64 AMD EPYC-Rome processor. It features 16 vCPUs across 16 physical cores running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 128.0 GB of memory and a bundled 1600 GB NVMe SSD. While the CPU allocation is shared, the instance delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks and strong memory performance, ranking in the top 25% of PassMark memory tests. The high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core and substantial local storage make this configuration highly efficient for memory-intensive applications. It is best suited for database hosting, caching layers, and memory-heavy workloads that require fast local storage but do not demand dedicated CPU cores.
og_description: Vultr voc-m-16c-128gb-1600s-amd is an AMD EPYC-Rome server with 16 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 1600 GB NVMe storage, offering strong memory performance for database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Vultr voc-m-16c-128gb-1600s-amd is an AMD EPYC-Rome server with 16 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 1600 GB NVMe SSD storage for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: An AMD-powered cloud server featuring high memory density, fast local NVMe storage, and top-tier single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC-Rome architecture with 16 vCPUs and 16 physical cores
128 GB of system memory providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio
Bundled 1600 GB NVMe SSD local storage
Top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong memory benchmark scores
Shared CPU allocation suitable for burstable or moderate compute needs
Optimized for database hosting, caching layers, and memory-heavy applications
